page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-90-lssd is a server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 90 dedicated vCPUs derived from 45 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 360.0 GB of RAM, providing a resource density of 4.0 GB per core. Storage is bundled with 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD capacity spread across 8 local SSDs. No GPUs are included in this configuration, and it contains zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 129166.19 and a PassMark CPU score of 87474.41. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration (AES, VAES, SHA_NI), resulting in top-tier performance in PassMark CPU extended instructions and encryption tests. In memory benchmarks, read-only bandwidth is strong, reaching 6372.53 GB/sec for small cached blocks and 217.44 GB/sec for large blocks, though large block write bandwidth is average at 76.89 GB/sec.

This instance demonstrates high efficiency for database and storage-heavy workloads, supported by a Redis SET throughput of 117.73 million operations per second and strong PassMark database operations. Software compilation and ray tracing benchmarks also rank in the top tier. While prompt processing for small and medium language models is strong to top-tier, text generation for large models (70B) is average at 4.79 tokens per second, indicating a tradeoff when running massive generative AI workloads without dedicated GPU acceleration. The hardware profile is suitable for database hosting, software compilation pipelines, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-90-lssd is an x86_64 server featuring 90 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor, 360.0 GB of RAM, and 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, as evidenced by a PassMark CPU score of 87474.41 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 129166.19. The instance excels in database operations, achieving 117.73 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, and demonstrates strong memory read bandwidth. However, large block write bandwidth and large-model LLM text generation represent performance tradeoffs. This server is configured for database hosting, software compilation, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-90-lssd is an x86_64 server with 90 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and 3216 GB local NVMe SSD. It delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-90-lssd features 90 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 360 GB RAM, and 3216 GB local NVMe SSD for database and compute workloads.
tagline: A high-density compute and storage instance featuring ninety dedicated AMD EPYC cores and three terabytes of local NVMe SSD.
bullet_points: 90 dedicated vCPUs powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors.
360 GB of system memory paired with 3216 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and software compilation speeds.
Exceptional Redis throughput of 117.73 million operations per second.
Suited for database hosting, heavy multi-threaded workloads, and web serving.
